reasoning: The Indeed US Job Postings index has been on a steady downward trend over the past year, recently dropping roughly 0.03 to 0.05 points per day. This strong momentum strongly suggests the index will continue to fall in the immediate term. For the 1-week (May 18) and 1-month (June 10) horizons, we expect the value to fall below the May 11 baseline of 105.74, yielding low probabilities of an increase. However, the index is approaching its pre-pandemic baseline of 100, and ongoing labor market cooling may soon stabilize, aided by Federal Reserve rate cuts and a "soft landing" macroeconomic scenario. Therefore, as the forecast horizon extends to 3 months (August 9) and 6 months (November 7), the uncertainty surrounding the continuation of this downtrend increases. Mean-reversion and the potential for new economic cycles pull the probabilities for these distant dates incrementally closer to 0.5.
